proposed_hypotheses.text: PFS (pfs_months) is positively correlated with feature_015 (continuous, range 30-90).
PFS decreases as feature_014 increases (ordinal 0/1/2).
Patients with feature_006='squamous' have shorter PFS than those with feature_006='adenocarcinoma'.
PFS varies by feature_001 (smoking status: current/former/never), with current smokers having the shortest PFS.

analyses.code: from scipy.stats import pearsonr; pearsonr(df.feature_015, df.pfs_months)
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r=+0.757 between feature_015 and pfs_months (very strong positive association).
Mean PFS: feature_014=0 -> 4.33, =1 -> 3.19, =2 -> 2.15. Linear trend strongly negative.
Mean PFS: squamous 2.81 vs adenocarcinoma 3.67. Squamous patients have shorter PFS.
Mean PFS: current 2.99, former 3.61, never 3.67. Current smokers have markedly shorter PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_023=1 have shorter PFS than those with feature_023=0.
Patients with feature_011=1 have shorter PFS than those with feature_011=0.
Patients with feature_016=1 have longer PFS than those with feature_016=0.
Patients with feature_028=1 have shorter PFS than those with feature_028=0.

analyses.result_summary: feature_023=1 PFS=2.50 vs =0 PFS=3.94. Strong negative effect.
feature_011=1 PFS=2.74 vs =0 PFS=3.66. Strong negative effect.
feature_016=1 PFS=4.13 vs =0 PFS=3.34. Strong positive effect.
feature_028=1 PFS=2.66 vs =0 PFS=3.47. Strong negative effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_017 (continuous, 0-28) is negatively associated with PFS.
feature_025 (continuous, 1.7-5.5) is positively associated with PFS.
feature_032 (continuous, ~50-830) is negatively associated with PFS.
feature_018 (binary, 35% prevalence) is positively associated with PFS.

analyses.result_summary: r=-0.127 between feature_017 and PFS, modest but highly significant negative association.
r=+0.099, modest positive association.
r=-0.018, weak but significant negative association in this large sample.
feature_018=1 PFS=3.65 vs =0 PFS=3.32. Moderate positive effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After joint adjustment in a multivariable linear regression for all features, the dominant independent predictors of PFS are feature_015 (+), feature_014 (-), feature_023 (-), feature_011 (-), feature_016 (+), feature_018 (+), feature_025 (+), feature_017 (-), and feature_006=squamous (-).
Many univariate-significant features (e.g., feature_022, feature_028, feature_021, feature_002, feature_012) are confounded and lose independent significance after adjustment.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ all features + C(feature_014) + C(feature_001) + C(feature_006)', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: OLS R^2=0.894. Independent coefficients (months PFS): feature_015 +0.168/unit (t=512), feature_014=1 -1.15, =2 -2.18, feature_023 -1.42, feature_011 -0.91, feature_016 +0.89, feature_018 +0.32, feature_025 +0.46/unit, feature_017 -0.073/unit, feature_006[squamous] -0.72, current smoker -0.55, feature_031 -0.20, feature_005 -0.11. Model explains nearly 90% of PFS variance.

feature_022 (p=0.94), feature_028 (p=0.06), feature_002 (p=0.04, tiny effect), feature_012 (p=0.38), feature_020 (p=0.71), feature_027 (p=0.87), feature_021 (p=0.46) lost significance after adjustment - their univariate associations were driven by confounding.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_022 is enriched in never-smokers (i.e., it represents a smoking-related biomarker like an EGFR-pattern mutation).
feature_028 is enriched in never-smokers but at lower prevalence than feature_022 (resembling an ALK-pattern marker).

feature_018, feature_011, feature_023, feature_002, feature_005, feature_012, feature_020, and feature_027 are statistically independent of all patient features (consistent with randomized treatment-like assignment), while feature_022 and feature_028 are correlated with histology and smoking.

analyses.result_summary: feature_022 prevalence: never 51.3%, former 8.1%, current 3.0%. Strongly enriched in never-smokers.
feature_028 prevalence: never 10.3%, former 4.8%, current 2.6%. Enriched in never-smokers, lower overall.

analyses.code: Cross-tabulation of binary features against feature_006, feature_001, feature_023, feature_014; chi-square tests
analyses.result_summary: feature_018, feature_011, feature_023, feature_002, feature_005, feature_012, feature_020, feature_027 are uniformly distributed across histology, smoking, age, performance status (max difference <1%). feature_022 and feature_028 strongly track smoking and histology. feature_016 and feature_021 partially track smoking. The independent ones behave like randomized assignment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the random-like binary features, feature_018 (positive main effect of +0.32 months) is the most plausible candidate for an effective therapeutic intervention; we expect to find substantial treatment-effect heterogeneity.
feature_011 and feature_023 appear to act as approximately constant negative-effect indicators, so we expect their effects to be similar across patient strata.

analyses.code: Conceptual triage based on independence + main-effect direction
analyses.result_summary: feature_018 chosen as primary candidate for heterogeneity probing.

analyses.code: Plan to verify constancy across (016, 028) strata in later iterations
analyses.result_summary: Hypothesis to test in iteration 14.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive interaction between feature_018 and feature_016: the PFS benefit of feature_018 is larger in patients with feature_016=1.
There is a negative interaction between feature_018 and feature_001=never smoker: the PFS benefit of feature_018 is smaller in never smokers.
There is a negative interaction between feature_018 and feature_022 (EGFR-pattern marker): feature_018 provides less benefit in feature_022=1 patients.
There is a negative interaction between feature_018 and feature_028 (ALK-pattern marker): feature_018 provides less benefit in feature_028=1 patients.
There is a positive interaction between feature_018 and feature_006=squamous: feature_018 provides more benefit in squamous histology.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient feature_018:feature_016 = +2.558, p<<0.001. Massive synergy: feature_018 benefit is huge when feature_016=1.
Interaction feature_018:never = -0.497, feature_018:former = -0.211 (current is reference). Treatment benefit is reduced in former and never smokers.
Interaction feature_018:feature_022 = -0.241, p=9.9e-05. feature_022+ patients gain less from feature_018.
Interaction feature_018:feature_028 = -0.488, p=4.1e-07. feature_028+ patients gain substantially less from feature_018.
Interaction feature_018:squamous = +0.171, p=1.9e-04. Modest positive interaction with squamous histology.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Stratifying by feature_016, the feature_018 PFS benefit is concentrated entirely in feature_016=1 patients (treatment effect is essentially 0 when feature_016=0).

analyses.code: Subgroup t-tests of feature_018 within each feature_016 stratum
analyses.result_summary: feature_016=0 (n=43,632): feature_018 effect = -0.005 months (p=0.80, no benefit). feature_016=1 (n=6,368): feature_018 effect = +2.552 months (p=2.5e-275, large benefit). Heterogeneity is qualitative, not just quantitative.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the feature_016=1 stratum, feature_028 further modifies the feature_018 benefit: feature_018 helps in feature_016=1, feature_028=0 but is null/harmful in feature_016=1, feature_028=1.

analyses.code: Subgroup t-tests of feature_018 in feature_016=1 stratified by feature_028
analyses.result_summary: feature_016=1, feature_028=0 (n=6,109): feature_018 effect = +2.667 months (p=3.4e-291). feature_016=1, feature_028=1 (n=259): feature_018 effect = -0.349 months (p=0.23, not significant). feature_028=1 essentially abolishes the treatment benefit even in the marker-positive subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within feature_016=1, feature_028=0, an additional binary feature among {feature_022, feature_021, feature_023, feature_011, feature_002, feature_005, feature_012, feature_020, feature_027, feature_031} further modifies the feature_018 effect.

analyses.code: Stratified t-tests of feature_018 within feature_016=1, feature_028=0, by each remaining binary feature
analyses.result_summary: Among binary modifiers: feature_031 produces the largest stratification - feature_031=0 (n=3,370): feature_018 effect = +4.832 months (p<<0.001); feature_031=1 (n=2,739): feature_018 effect = +0.002 months (p=0.98). Other binaries (feature_022, 021, 023, 011, 002, 012, 020, 027) preserve a ~+2.5-2.7 month effect in both strata. feature_005=1 attenuates the effect to +0.20 (p=0.56) but n=183 is small.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The complete subgroup definition for feature_018 PFS benefit is: feature_016=1 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_031=0; outside this subgroup, feature_018 has no effect on PFS.

analyses.code: Cross-tab of feature_018 effect across all 8 combos of (feature_016, feature_028, feature_031)

proposed_hypotheses.text: A four-way interaction model feature_018 * feature_016 * feature_028 * feature_031 confirms the subgroup definition: the only large positive interaction term is feature_018:feature_016, and feature_028=1 or feature_031=1 each cancel that benefit.

analyses.result_summary: Key terms: feature_018:feature_016 = +4.85 (p<1e-300); feature_018:feature_016:feature_028 = -5.01 (p=1.8e-34); feature_018:feature_016:feature_031 = -4.86 (p<1e-300); 4-way = +4.74 (p=2.4e-15). Interpretation: in feature_016=1 alone, treatment effect is +4.85 months; either feature_028=1 or feature_031=1 essentially zeroes it out.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Other random-like binary features (feature_002, feature_005, feature_012, feature_020, feature_027) show no meaningful treatment-effect heterogeneity with the standard biomarkers and patient features.

analyses.code: Pairwise ols(pfs_months ~ feat * modifier) for each remaining candidate against feature_019, 006, 001, 022, 028, 021, 017, 015, 025, 032, 014, 016
analyses.result_summary: feature_002, feature_020, feature_027: no interactions with p<0.001. feature_005 has small interaction with feature_016 (-0.82, p=2.8e-06) but main effect is also small and prevalence only 3% so practical impact is limited. feature_012 has tiny interaction with feature_017 (+0.018/unit, p=3.2e-04). None approach the magnitude or specificity of feature_018 x feature_016.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_011 acts as a constant negative prognostic factor: its effect (~-0.9 months) is similar across (feature_016, feature_028) strata - not a treatment with heterogeneity.
feature_023 acts as a constant negative prognostic factor: its effect (~-1.4 months) is similar across (feature_016, feature_028) strata.
feature_011 and feature_023 are additive (no interaction).

analyses.code: Subgroup t-tests of feature_011 across (feature_016, feature_028) strata
Subgroup t-tests of feature_023 across (feature_016, feature_028) strata

analyses.result_summary: Interaction term coef = -0.012, p=0.79. Strictly additive.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the feature_018 responder subgroup (feature_016=1, feature_028=0, feature_031=0), smoking status (feature_001) does not strongly modify the feature_018 benefit.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_018*C(feature_001)', sub_016_028) where sub is feature_016=1 and feature_028=0
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_016=1, feature_028=0: feature_018 main effect = +2.54 in current (reference); :former = +0.28 (p=0.04); :never = -0.79 (p=0.10). Modest modification, none strongly significant after multiple comparisons. Smoking does not meaningfully reshape the subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Continuous prognostic factors feature_017 and feature_025 retain independent significance after adjustment but do not modify the feature_018 treatment effect.

analyses.result_summary: feature_018 x feature_017 interaction p=0.99 (no modification). feature_018 x feature_025 interaction p=0.16 (no modification). Continuous prognostic factors act additively with treatment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_018 PFS effect is not modified by feature_023 (likely stage marker) or feature_011 (other prognostic feature) within the responder subgroup.

analyses.code: Stratified t-tests of feature_018 within (016=1,028=0) by feature_023 and by feature_011
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_016=1, feature_028=0: feature_023=0 effect=+2.71; feature_023=1 effect=+2.63 (similar). feature_011=0 effect=+2.75; feature_011=1 effect=+2.44 (similar). Treatment benefit holds regardless of these other prognostic factors.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Restricting to the full responder subgroup (feature_016=1, feature_028=0, feature_031=0), the absolute PFS gain from feature_018 (treated 8.09 vs untreated 3.26 months) is approximately +4.83 months.

analyses.result_summary: n=3370 patients; treated mean PFS = 8.09 months (n=1191); untreated mean PFS = 3.26 months (n=2179). Difference = +4.83 months, t-test p<<1e-300.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The continuous candidate biomarker feature_019 (range 0-0.8, plausibly PD-L1-like) does NOT modify the feature_018 effect after accounting for feature_016.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_018*feature_019', df) and within feature_016=1 stratum
analyses.result_summary: Whole cohort feature_018:feature_019 interaction p=0.32. Within feature_016=1, p=0.08. feature_019 does not act as the treatment-effect modifier - feature_016 captures the predictive biomarker.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_005 (rare binary, 3% prevalence) within feature_016=1, feature_028=0 abolishes the feature_018 effect (consistent with a fourth resistance marker).

analyses.code: Stratified t-test of feature_018 within (016=1, 028=0) by feature_005
analyses.result_summary: feature_005=0 (n=5926): feature_018 effect = +2.74 months. feature_005=1 (n=183): feature_018 effect = +0.20 months (p=0.56). Suggestive resistance signal but very small subgroup; included in transcript with caveat.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_001='current smoker' have shorter PFS than former smokers independently of all other features.
feature_006='squamous' is independently associated with shorter PFS after adjustment.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS coefficients on smoking dummies
analyses.result_summary: current smoker coef = -0.545 (p<1e-100), former smoker coef = +0.006 (n.s.). Current smoking is the bad category; former smoking is essentially equivalent to never.
feature_006[squamous] coef = -0.723 (t=-95). Independently strong negative effect even after adjusting for everything else.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_015 is the strongest single predictor of PFS in this cohort and is positively associated with PFS.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coef = +0.168 months PFS per unit feature_015 (t=512). Net effect across the 60-unit feature range is ~+10 months. Note: positive direction is unusual if feature_015 is age, suggesting either inverted encoding or that the variable is not age (could be a dose/intensity-like covariate).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Final best-supported treatment-effect subgroup hypothesis: feature_018 increases PFS by ~+4.8 months exclusively in patients with feature_016=1 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_031=0; feature_018 has no detectable effect (point estimates near zero, p>0.2) in any other subgroup.

analyses.code: Final subgroup verification: 8-cell cross-tab of feature_018 effect by (feature_016, feature_028, feature_031)
analyses.result_summary: Only the (016=1, 028=0, 031=0) cell shows significant positive effect (+4.83 months, p~0, n=3370). All seven other cells: effect within +/-0.4 months, all p>0.2. feature_016 is the positive predictive biomarker; feature_028 and feature_031 each act as resistance/non-response markers that nullify benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_011 and feature_023 are simple negative prognostic factors with constant additive effects (not treatments with heterogeneity); their effects (-0.9 and -1.4 months respectively) are approximately invariant across patient subgroups.

analyses.code: Sweep of feature_011 and feature_023 effects across all (016, 028, 031) cells; pairwise interaction tests against all features.
analyses.result_summary: feature_011 effect across cells: -0.7 to -1.0 (no qualitative variation). feature_023 effect across cells: -1.0 to -1.5. No feature produces a >0.5-month, p<0.001 interaction with either. Both are best characterized as constant negative prognostic factors rather than treatments.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Final summary model: PFS = baseline (driven by feature_015, feature_014, feature_023, feature_011, feature_006, feature_001, feature_017, feature_025) + treatment-effect heterogeneity for feature_018 captured by interaction with feature_016, feature_028, feature_031.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_015 + feature_017 + feature_025 + feature_032 + feature_023 + feature_011 + C(feature_014) + C(feature_006) + C(feature_001) + feature_018*feature_016*feature_028*feature_031', df)
analyses.result_summary: Combined model achieves R^2 ~ 0.91, with feature_018 effect localized to the feature_016=1, feature_028=0, feature_031=0 cell (interaction terms as in iter 12). All baseline prognostic factors retain their expected signs and magnitudes. Final treatment-effect heterogeneity story for the only outcome (pfs_months) is complete and clinically interpretable: feature_018 is a treatment with biomarker-defined response (feature_016+) and two resistance modifiers (feature_028 and feature_031).
